wikitext:
[]: 303 Post by evaunit13 » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:06 pm something i thought about when lying down and looking at my clock. Asuka's hospital room was 303. 303 upside down is EOE, an acronym we know so well. did anno choose some random number or was it deliberate?

[]: Post by esselfortium » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:10 pm He was harking back to the golden age of acid house.

[]: Post by Gamer137 » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:13 pm After seeing Misato and Shinji in a parking garage area numbered 667, making the last area they were in 666, I wouldn't be surprised if it was probably was another use symbolism.

[]: Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:47 pm Should have been 404, indicating Asuka wasn't currently available. Doubly symbolic since 4 is a number almost never used in Japanese hospitals on account of wordplay that equates it with death.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 5:15 pm 303 sideways is "MOM". It plays into the oedipal thing. It's also "WOW", as in... well...

[]: Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jul 23, 2010 5:21 pm NAveryW wrote: 303 sideways is "MOM". It plays into the oedipal thing. It's also "WOW", as in... well...

[]: Re: 303 Post by Smugleaf » Fri Jul 23, 2010 6:03 pm evaunit13 wrote: something i thought about when lying down and looking at my clock. Asuka's hospital room was 303. 303 upside down is EOE, an acronym we know so well. did anno choose some random number or was it deliberate?

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 23, 2010 6:03 pm NAveryW wrote: It's also "WOW", as in... well...

[]: Re: 303 Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 6:14 pm Smugleaf wrote: guys c'mon is not like anno planned every detail of evangelion

[]: Post by BobBQ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 6:21 pm Asuka's room number is obviously a classy reference to what the SSDF would have done with her if she hadn't been loaded into Eva-02. "We applied Rule Three-Oh-Three! We caught them and we shot them under Rule Three-Oh-Three!" </thread>

[]: Re: 303 Post by Smugleaf » Fri Jul 23, 2010 6:45 pm NAveryW wrote: But someone did, whether or not it was Anno. The numbers may or not mean anything, but numbers in Eva often do. The Type 666 firewall , the 108 dummy companies of the Marduk Institute, the spaceship XL5-1701, etc. Remember that when you're making an animation, you have to come up with everything, so everything is there for a reason. That reason can just be "These are the first numbers that came to mind," or it can be "I just love cramming as many references to old sci-fi into my anime as I can". I do think the EOE thing is a stretch, though. MOM? Maybe, but we have no way of knowing for sure, so even if it is the case it may as well not be.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 7:50 pm If Anno didn't want people to search for hidden details, he wouldn't put them all over the frakking place. Just in terms of numbers, 666 is an obvious reference to the Number of the Beast, as is the later 667; 108, which is used both as the number of dummy companies for Marduk and the step Gendo says to start over from when Unit 01 rejects the Dummy Plug, is a significant number in Buddhism that appears all over the place (108 sins, 108 beads on a rosary, 108 earthly temptations to overcome to reach nirvana); XL5-1701 is a reference to Fireball XL5 and the NCC-1701 from Star Trek ; the number of Seele monoliths in NGE is (ostensibly) 12, which is the number of Christ's apostles; the seven eyes on the Seele logo... well, seven is all over Christianity, but in this case it most likely refers to the seven "eyes of God"; Unit 00 is Rei's Evangelion, and zero is "Rei" in Japanese... Anything I left out? Oh, right, right. The 0⁹ system. According to Reichu, though I can't personally confirm this, it's a pun on "onani", the Japanese word for "masturbation". ADV thinks it's a pun on "oni".

[]: Post by esselfortium » Fri Jul 23, 2010 8:46 pm NAveryW wrote: Anything I left out? Oh, right, right. The 0⁹ system. According to Reichu, though I can't personally confirm this, it's a pun on "onani", the Japanese word for "masturbation". ADV thinks it's a pun on "oni".

[]: Re: 303 Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:11 pm Hehe, I really like this thread. It plays with the idea of visual semiotics: I dig. 303 = WOW = EOE = MOM - What more could you want than a full 360-degrees of meaning out of a number? Smugleaf wrote: YOU HAVE TO BE KIDDING ME NOT EVERYTHING IN EVANGELION IS SYMBOLIC!!!! GOD DAMM ANNO IS NOT A MACHINE OF LOLSYMBOLISM

[]: Re: 303 Post by Smugleaf » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:25 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Hehe, I really like this thread. It plays with the idea of visual semiotics: I dig. 303 = WOW = EOE = MOM - What more could you want than a full 360-degrees of meaning out of a number? The number symbolism in NGE is peanuts compared to what's in Futurama. It's ridiculously easy to insert relevant/symbolic numbers/letters into works like this, really. It boggles my mind that you've come to a dedicated NGE forum expecting that this kind of discussion wouldn't exist... I don't get it... what did you expect we'd be doing? It's stupidly easy to stay out of these threads if you don't like what we're doing...

[]: Re: 303 Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:29 pm Smugleaf wrote: i did expect this kinds of discussions but god i didn't expect that you would put symbolisms in every frame of evangelion i mean why you can't think mmh random number is random it doesn't have to be MOM or EoE its nonsensical and yet is more easy to not create topics about every frame of animation as i posted in my now closed tread you overanalyze everything, to be honest yes evangelion have a lot of symbolic bullshit but it seems that you think that those symbolisms are in every part of the animation for god's sake someone started a tread about the seawaves that we see in rebuild because those are recycled of EoE and he tought that it was a confirmation of the sequel theory isn't that just WAY TOO MUCH?

[]: Re: 303 Post by Smugleaf » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:32 pm NAveryW wrote: Congratulations, you've won the prize for longest run-on sentence on EGF! I think I speak for everyone when I say [redacted]

[]: Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:39 pm You have no argument. You just repeated the same stuff as before. The fact that not every frame is symbolic/significant doesn't mean that any given frame can't be, especially since Evangelion has consistently shown itself to choose its numbers carefully. As for my opinion, whether or not 303 was intended to be significant, it doesn't matter because it's impossible to tell for sure. I wouldn't be surprised either way.

[]: Post by Smugleaf » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:41 pm NAveryW wrote: You have no argument. You just repeated the same stuff as before. As for my opinion, whether or not 303 was intended to be significant, it doesn't matter because it's impossible to tell for sure. I wouldn't be surprised either way.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:44 pm A lot of analysis is overanalysis, yes. It may be overanalysis in this case as well, but I think immediately writing it off as overanalysis is a bad idea. I've given many examples of numbers in Eva with significance attached to them. What makes you sure 303 is different? And as Yojimbo said, Futurama is full of this sort of thing too. The main difference is Futurama has audio commentaries that explain the meanings.
